Let me look at the structure of the binary more closely, especially for any encoded data sections.

The title says "AES_Encrypted_Shell" - maybe the XOR is just the first layer and the actual shellcode contains AES-encrypted data that gets decrypted at runtime. Or maybe the payload is encrypted with AES and the XOR is part of a larger scheme.

But from the disassembly, all we can see is XOR decryption. Let me look for AES-related code elsewhere in the binary. The binary is statically linked with glibc, which doesn't include OpenSSL/libcrypto AES functions. Unless custom AES is implemented.

Let me search for AES-related constants (like S-box values) in the binary.
